steps.title: Isolate the square root term
steps.content:
Subtract 37 from both sides to isolate $\sqrt{w}$:
$$\sqrt{w} + 37 = 47$$$$\sqrt{w} = 47 - 37$$$$\sqrt{w} = 10$$

steps.title: Square both sides to solve for w
steps.content:
Square both sides to eliminate the square root and solve for $w$:
$$(\sqrt{w})^2 = 10^2$$$$w = 100$$
Verify by substituting back: $\sqrt{100} + 37 = 10 + 37 = 47$ ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
